Role in Deterministic Routing: Primary decision engine for physical location and high-level policy enforcement.
Key Artifacts & Rules: repo-manifest.hpc.json: Routes objectKind+tier to targetRepo, pathPattern, schemaWhitelist. Policy flags include OneFilePerRequest, RequireDeadLedgerRef. Can be extended with aiAuthoringRules and authoringHints.

Role in Deterministic Routing: Definitive catalog of all legal invariants, metrics, and their permissible bands across tiers. Acts as the ultimate authority on quantitative constraints.
Key Artifacts & Rules: invariants-spine, entertainment-metrics-spine: Defines valid ranges, tier overrides, and cross-metric interactions for all governance variables.

Role in Deterministic Routing: Orchestrator that normalizes intent and resolves all routing and constraint information into a single, structured request envelope.
Key Artifacts & Rules: Accepts natural language or partial JSON. Outputs a fully populated chatDirectorRequestv1 with resolved objectKind, tier, schemaref, and a generationGuide.

Role in Deterministic Routing: Optional, specialized layer providing canonical file path patterns for different objectKind and tier combinations to prevent path invention.
Key Artifacts & Rules: Maps objectKind+tier to canonical patterns (e.g., engine/*.lua, contracts/*.json). Used by the orchestrator to validate path construction.
